name: tailwind-sail
description: Tailwind sidebar for browsing, filtering, and editing class strings.

commands.title: Add Current File to Theme Files
Remove Current File from Theme Files

tailwind-sail.updateDebounceMs.markdownDescription: Delay in milliseconds before Tailwind Sail re-runs string detection and Tailwind parsing after cursor position or document changes.

tailwind-sail.highlightActiveString.markdownDescription: Underline the active string literal Tailwind Sail is using in the editor.

enumDescriptions: Merge every `@apply` in the enclosing rule body (Tailwind merges them when building). Sidebar and underlines reflect the combined list.
Merge only the `@apply` directive that contains the caret (including multi-line parameters). Sidebar and highlights stay scoped to that single directive.
tailwind-sail.applyAtCaretScope.markdownDescription: For CSS/SCSS `@apply`: whether Tailwind Sail treats the whole rule body as one editable surface or only the `@apply` the caret is inside.

tailwind-sail.layout.markdownDescription: Horizontal inset for sidebar content: **loose** or **compact**.

tailwind-sail.paddingTop.markdownDescription: Top padding above sidebar content: **loose** or **compact**.

tailwind-sail.showSidebarRightBorder.markdownDescription: Draw a 1px right border on the Tailwind Sail webview. Off by default for a cleaner look in VS Code; turn on in hosts such as Cursor if the auxiliary bar needs a visible separator.

tailwind-sail.showUtilityPreview.markdownDescription: Show color swatches and raw value hints next to each class in the sidebar. Turn off for a denser list.

tailwind-sail.saveDocumentAfterEdit.markdownDescription: After a successful Tailwind Sail edit, save the active editor document: removing a class, adding a class (Enter in **Add**), stepping the named scale chevrons, or pressing **Enter** in a class token field (live edits are already applied). Does not save on every keystroke while typing a token.

tailwind-sail.variables.sourceFiles.markdownDescription: Workspace-relative paths to `.css` theme files scanned for custom properties (`--name: value`). Non-`.css` paths are ignored. Use the commands **Add Current File to Theme Files** / **Remove Current File from Theme Files**, or edit this list manually.

scripts.compile: pnpm run compile:ui && tsc -p ./ && pnpm run bundle:ext
scripts.pretest: pnpm run compile && pnpm run lint
